家族.mnemonics: Think of 'kazoku' as 'ka-zone-cook.' Imagine your family cooking together in a cozy kitchen zone.

おじいさん.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, gee, son!' Picture an old man saying this to his grandson.

おばあさん.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, bah, son!' Imagine a grandmother knitting and saying this.

お兄さん.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, knee, son!' Imagine an older brother who always kneeled down to tie your shoelaces.

お姉さん.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, neigh, son!' Imagine an older sister who loves horses and often mimics a horse's neigh.

父.mnemonics: 'Chichi' sounds like 'chee-chee.' Picture your father playing a game of chess ('chee' for chess).

母.mnemonics: Sounds like laughter 'ha-ha.' Remember your mother's laugh.

兄.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Ah, knee.' Imagine your older brother always hitting his knee on the table.

姉.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Ah, neh.' Imagine your older sister pondering and saying 'Ah, neh' when thinking.

妹.mnemonics: Sounds like 'E-moto.' Picture your younger sister riding an e-motorbike.

弟.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, toe-toe.' Imagine your younger brother always stubbing his toe.

兄弟.mnemonics: Think of 'kyo-die.' Picture siblings playing a video game together and saying, 'If you lose (die) in Kyoto...'

男の人.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, toko no hito.' Imagine a man saying, 'Oh, I'm just a 'toko' (guy) in this place.'

女の人.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, nonna hito.' Picture a woman saying, 'Oh, I'm nonna (not a) regular person.'

会社.mnemonics: Sounds like 'kai-sha.' Imagine a company on the coast (kai) near the sea.

食堂.mnemonics: Sounds like 'shoku-dough.' Picture a cafeteria where they always bake fresh dough.

デパート.mnemonics: Sounds like 'depart.' Think of a place where you always depart with your money.

眼鏡.mnemonics: Sounds like 'May-gah-neh.' Picture someone in May going 'Neh' because they can't see without their glasses.

歌.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ooh-tah.' Imagine saying 'ooh' in wonder when you hear a beautiful song.

club activity.mnemonics: Sounds like 'circle.' Picture a group of people forming a circle for a club meeting.

車.mnemonics: Sounds like 'cool-ma.' Imagine a really cool mom driving a fancy car.

長い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'nag-eye.' Think of a long, nagging sigh.

短い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'me-gee-guy.' Imagine a short guy who always says 'Me? Gee, I'm short.'

速い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'high-yai.' Think of something moving so fast it goes high and yells 'Yai!'

背が高い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sega-ta-kai.' Picture a tall person who loves playing Sega games.

背が低い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sega-hikui.' Imagine a short person who feels low (hikui) when they can't reach the Sega console.

頭がいい.mnemonics: Sounds like 'atama guy E.' Picture a smart guy named E who always uses his head (atama).

可愛い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'kawaii.' This is similar to the English word 'cawaii' for cute.

親切.mnemonics: Sounds like 'shin-sets.' Picture a kind person who always sets their heart (shin) on doing good.

便利.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ben-ri.' Imagine Ben, who is always handy and convenient to have around.

歌う.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ooh-ta-oo.' Picture singing a song with 'ooh' sounds.

被る.mnemonics: Sounds like 'cab-ru.' Imagine putting on a hat before getting into a cab.

english: to put on items below your waist
履く.mnemonics: Sounds like 'huck.' Think of 'hucking' on your pants or shoes.

知る.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sheer.' Picture getting to know something as clearly as seeing through sheer fabric.

知っています.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sheet-tea-mass.' Imagine confidently saying 'I know' as you sip a cup of tea.

知りません.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sheer-ma-sen.' Picture saying 'I don't know' with a sheer look of confusion.

住む.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sum.' Think of where you live as the sum of your home life.

働く.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ha-ta-laku.' Imagine working hard while saying 'Ha! Tackle this work!'

太る.mnemonics: Sounds like 'futo-ru.' Imagine gaining weight and saying 'Futo, I grew!'

english: to be on the heavy side/overweight
太っています.mnemonics: Sounds like 'futo-tte-mass.' Picture someone who's heavy saying, 'Futo, I'm mass!'

眼鏡を掛ける.mnemonics: Sounds like 'may-gah-neh-wo-kakeru.' Imagine putting on glasses to see the month of May clearly.

english: to put on clothes above your waist
着る.mnemonics: Sounds like 'keer.' Picture 'keering' a shirt over your head.

痩せる.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ya-sell.' Imagine losing weight and saying, 'Ya, I can sell my old clothes now!'

痩せています.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ya-set-te-mass.' Picture a thin person saying, 'Ya, I'm set with this mass!'

結婚する.mnemonics: Sounds like 'kett-kon-suru.' Picture getting married and setting a 'ket' (contract) to connect lives.

何も.mnemonics: Sounds like 'nani mo.' Picture saying 'Nani? Mo nothing?'

～人.mnemonics: Sounds like 'neen.' Imagine counting people and saying 'One neen, two neen...'

一人.mnemonics: Sounds like 'hitori.' Picture being alone and saying 'Hi, I'm just one here.'

二人.mnemonics: Sounds like 'futari.' Think of 'two' (fu-ta-ri) people standing together.

別に.mnemonics: Sounds like 'bet-su-ni.' Imagine shrugging and saying 'Bet, it's nothing special.'

勿論.mnemonics: Sounds like 'mochi-ron.' Picture saying 'Of course!' while eating mochi.

english: to put on items below your waist
はく.mnemonics: Sounds like 'huck.' Think of 'hucking' on your pants or shoes.

～が.mnemonics: Think of it as a pause, like saying 'ga...' when you're about to say 'but...'

よかったら.mnemonics: Sounds like 'yokatta-ra.' Imagine saying, 'If you like (yokatta), then ra (come on)!'

目.mnemonics: Sounds like 'me.' Picture 'me' focusing my eyes to see clearly.

鼻.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Hana.' Imagine a flower ('hana' in Japanese) near your nose.

耳.mnemonics: Sounds like 'me-me.' Think of someone saying 'Listen to me' with their ears.

口.mnemonics: Sounds like 'coochie.' Remember by thinking of a baby's coochie-coo, often said while touching their mouth.

歯.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ha.' Imagine laughing 'ha-ha' and showing your teeth.

眉毛.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Mayu-ge.' Picture drawing your eyebrows carefully in May.

髪.mnemonics: Think of 'kami' as in origami. Picture folding origami with hair-like delicacy.

頭.mnemonics: Sounds like 'a-tama.' Imagine a head ('atama') adorned with a crown.

顔.mnemonics: Sounds like 'cow-o.' Picture a face with the calmness of a cow's expression.

首.mnemonics: Think of 'kubi' as in 'cubicle.' Imagine stretching your neck looking over cubicle walls.

肩.mnemonics: Sounds like 'cutter.' Imagine a strong shoulder cutting through the water in swimming.

手.mnemonics: 'Te' as in 'tea.' Picture yourself delicately holding a tea cup in your hand.

指.mnemonics: Sounds like 'you-be.' Think of pointing a finger and saying, 'You be careful.'

胸.mnemonics: Sounds like 'mooneh.' Picture a treasure chest, as valuable as the moon.

背中.mnemonics: Sounds like 'say-naka.' Imagine someone saying 'Naka' (middle) while pointing at their back.

おなか.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh! Naka.' Picture feeling a sudden stomachache and exclaiming 'Oh, naka (inside)!'

尾尻.mnemonics: Sounds like 'oh-she-ree.' Think of sitting down after a long day saying, 'Oh, finally!'

足.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Ah-she.' Imagine stepping into a relaxing bath and saying 'Ah, relief!'
